zh.lifeSummary: 當Maud De Hastings於1337年出生在英國諾福爾可格雷哈爾時，她的父親Hugh Hastings28歲，母親Margery Foliot25歲。她與Sir Robert de la Mare結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1405年4月10日享年68歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Maud De Hastings于1337年出生在英国諾福爾可格雷哈爾时，她的父亲Hugh Hastings28岁，母亲Margery Foliot25岁。她与Sir Robert de la Mare结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1405年4月10日享年68岁。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Hastings
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from Hastings (Sussex), near which the English army was defeated by the Normans in 1066. The placename derives from an Old English personal name Hǣsta + the Old English groupname suffix -ingas. In the latter part of the 12th century, at the time of William the Lion, the surname was taken to Scotland, where it assimilated instances of the native Scottish surname Harestane.
English: variant of Hasting with Middle English genitival -s.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hOistín ‘descendant of Oistín’, the Gaelic form of the personal name Augustine (see Austin ).

variants.name: Hasting
meanings: English: from the Norman personal name Hastang, Hastenc, Hasten (Old Norse Hásteinn ‘high’ + ‘stone’). See also Hastings .

variants.name: Heston
meanings: English: habitational name from Heston (Middlesex) or Histon (Cambridgeshire). The Middlesex placename derives from Old English hǣs ‘brushwood’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, while the Cambridgeshire placename may derive from Old English hyse ‘shoot, tendril’, also ‘son, youth’, + tūn.

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Maud De Hastings was born in 1337, in Gressenhall, Norfolk, England, her father, Hugh Hastings, was 28 and her mother, Margery Foliot, was 25. She married Sir Robert de la Mare. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 10 April 1405, at the age of 68.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Maud De Hastings est née en 1337, à Gressenhall, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Hugh Hastings, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Margery Foliot, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Robert de la Mare. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 10 avril 1405, à l’âge de 68 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Maud De Hastings 1337 in Gressenhall, Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Hugh Hastings 28 und ihre Mutter Margery Foliot 25. Sie hat Sir Robert de la Mare geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 10. April 1405 im Alter von 68 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Maud De Hastings è nata il nel 1337 nel Gressenhall, Norfolk, England, sua padre, Hugh Hastings, aveva 28 anni e sua madre, Margery Foliot, aveva 25 anni. Lei sposato Sir Robert de la Mare. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 10 aprile 1405, all'età di 68 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Maud De Hastingsが1337年にイギリスノーフォルクグレセンハルで生まれた時，父Hugh Hastingsは28歳，母Margery Foliotは25歳であった。Sir Robert de la Mareと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1405年4月10日に68歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Maud De Hastings(이)가 1337년 영국 노르폴크 그레센홀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Hugh Hastings(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margery Foliot(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 Sir Robert de la Mare(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1405년 4월 10일 68세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Maud De Hastings nasceu em 1337, em Gressenhall, Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Hugh Hastings, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Margery Foliot, tinha 25 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Robert de la Mare. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 10 de abril de 1405, com 68 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Maud De Hastings родилась в 1337 г. в местности грессенолл, норфолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Hugh Hastings было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Margery Foliot, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Robert de la Mare. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 10 апреля 1405 г. в возрасте 68 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Maud De Hastings nació en 1337, en Gressenhall, Norfolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Hugh Hastings, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Margery Foliot, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Sir Robert de la Mare. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 10 de abril de 1405, a la edad de 68 años.
